tools_used: provisioned snapshot and record/documents (petition, QP, BIO)
helpful: CVSG cut in the statpack (27.1% grant vs 3.0%) — the single most decision-relevant number for this cell
Full extracted petition and brief-in-opposition text; the BIO's standing/vehicle sections drove the downweighting
Same-day snapshot with the complete proceedings list, including the CVSG entry
gaps: fedcourts query returned 0 rows for every topic tried (First Amendment / discrimination / bar association); topic retrieval seems to have little recall on doctrinal phrases, so no comparable priors could be pulled

Statpack has no salience-band table (the prompt describes one) and no CVSG-conditional detail such as time-to-disposition or SG-recommendation splits; a CVSG-specific cut of eventual outcomes by recommending administration would sharpen exactly this kind of cell
Cert-stage amicus counts are a known grant signal but are not a statpack cut
notes: Forward cell with a fresh same-day snapshot and full document text made external retrieval unnecessary; the corpus tooling's value here was the statpack, not query.
